questions.question: According to AFH 1, professionalism within the USAF is framed by which requirements?
options: Trust, loyalty, dignity, and personal commitment

questions.question: What is described as being at the root of professionalism?
The USAF is described as a Total Force that effectively leverages the unique capabilities of which groups?
options: Active duty, Reserve, and Guard members only
Officer, enlisted, and civilian Airmen across RegAF, Guard, Reserve, and Auxiliary Components
Officers and enlisted members across all military branches
Combat, support, and administrative personnel across all commands

questions.question: As a Total Force, the USAF is described as what type of air force?
options: A technology-driven, innovation-focused, results-oriented air force
A combat-ready, globally-engaged, strategically-minded air force
A values-based, mission-focused, people-oriented air force
A discipline-driven, service-focused, honor-bound air force

questions.question: Which Air Force Chief of Staff stated that members of the profession of arms are 'entrusted with the security of our nation, the protection of our citizens and the preservation of its way of life'?
General Ronald Fogleman was the _____ Air Force Chief of Staff.
According to AFH 1, the profession of arms is distinguished from other professions because of expertise in what area?
options: The justified application of lethal military force

questions.question: What are the three USAF core values?
options: Integrity First, Service Before Self, and Excellence In All We Do

questions.question: According to AFH 1, the USAF core values are recognized as universal, consistent standards used to evaluate what?
options: The combat readiness of all USAF units
The professional development of all Airmen
The ethical climate of all USAF organizations
The leadership effectiveness of all commanders

questions.question: What are the three virtues of Integrity First?
What are the three virtues of Service Before Self?
What are the three virtues of Excellence In All We Do?
According to AFH 1, integrity is described as what?
options: Placing the needs of the service above personal desires
A sustained passion for continuous improvement
Simply doing the right thing, all the time, whether everyone is watching, or no one is watching
An individual commitment to uphold the highest personal and professional standards

questions.question: According to AFH 1, courage is defined as:
options: The willingness to make personal sacrifices for the mission
Not the absence of fear but doing the right thing despite fear
The ability to lead others through difficult situations

questions.question: Which USAF core value represents an abiding dedication to the age-old military virtue of selfless dedication to duty?
According to AFH 1, loyalty as a virtue of Service Before Self is ordered in what priority?
options: Family first, unit second, nation third
The nation first, the values and commitments of the USAF second, and the men and women with whom we serve third
Self first, then peers, then the USAF
The mission first, commanders second, the nation third

questions.question: Excellence In All We Do does NOT mean:
options: Continuously advancing our craft and increasing knowledge
Demanding perfection in everything from everyone
Seeking out opportunities and completing developmental education
Having a passion for deliberate continuous improvement and innovation

questions.question: What is honesty described as in relation to integrity?
According to AFH 1, what are the three general causes or sources of ethical dilemmas?
options: Uncertainty, competing values, and potential harm
Ambiguity, conflicting orders, and time pressure

questions.question: Dr. Robert M. Hicks identified three tests to check the morality of actions and decisions. Which of the following is NOT one of those tests?
options: The United States of America Test

questions.question: The Network Test asks you to consider:
options: Whether your peers would approve of your decision
How your decision would look if aired on the news or social media
Whether your chain of command would support your decision
How your decision aligns with USAF regulations

questions.question: The Airman's Creed was presented to the USAF in 2007 by which Air Force Chief of Staff?
According to AFH 1, what are the three types of courage critical in the profession of arms?
According to AFH 1, what is the stated mission in the Airman's Creed?
According to Title 5, CFR, Part 2635, employees may accept voluntary gifts from subordinates of nominal value not to exceed what amount on occasions of special personal significance?
According to AFH 1, what is at the root of professionalism within the USAF?
options: Trust, loyalty, dignity, and personal commitment

questions.question: Enlisted promotion tests are designed to reflect each applicable Airman's knowledge. Group study for the purpose of enlisted promotion testing is:
options: Allowed only for study groups of three or fewer
Strictly prohibited in accordance with DAFMAN 36-2664

questions.question: According to AFH 1, enlisted personnel who violate the prohibition against group study for promotion testing are subject to prosecution under what article?
According to AFH 1, integrity is described as a compass that keeps us on the right path when confronted with what?
options: Difficult leadership decisions and organizational change

questions.question: According to AFH 1, accountability instills responsibility while maintaining what two qualities?
According to AFH 1, duty is defined as the obligation to perform what?
options: What is required for the mission
What is outlined in one's duty description

questions.question: According to AFH 1, discipline as a virtue of Excellence In All We Do is described as:
options: The ability to enforce standards across the organization
A willingness to follow orders without hesitation
A commander's tool for maintaining unit readiness
An individual commitment to uphold the highest of personal and professional standards

questions.question: According to AFH 1, whereas moral values describe what we hold to be right and wrong, ethics is:
options: A set of rules prescribed by military regulation
The systematic reflection of these values enabling us to live and act according to our beliefs
A legal framework for disciplinary proceedings
A code of conduct unique to each military branch

questions.question: According to AFH 1, laws only tell us what we can and cannot do. Ethics, on the other hand, tells us:
options: What we must do under military law
What we should or shouldn't do
What our commander expects of us

questions.question: The United States of America Test asks you to consider which prioritized questions?
options: Is this legal? Is this ethical? Is this moral?
Is this good for me? Is this good for my family? Is this good for my career?
Would my supervisor approve? Would my commander approve? Would the Secretary of the Air Force approve?
Is this good for the United States? Is this good for the USAF? Is this good for my unit? Is this good for me?

questions.question: The Divine Test deals with asking yourself which question?
options: Would my religious leader approve of this decision?
Would I feel good about the decision when I give account for my life?
Is this decision aligned with my spiritual beliefs?
Would I be comfortable discussing this decision with my family?

questions.question: According to AFH 1, valor is defined as:
options: The ability to face danger or hardship in a determined and resolute manner
The willingness to sacrifice one's life for the mission
A Medal of Honor-level act of heroism in combat
Physical bravery demonstrated exclusively on the battlefield

questions.question: According to AFH 1, personal courage is about:
options: Overcoming fears of bodily harm to get the job done
Standing by the core values when it may not be the popular thing to do
Doing what is right even when risking one's career
Demonstrating bravery in a combat environment

questions.question: According to AFH 1, moral courage is described as:
options: Doing what is right even when risking one's career
Overcoming fears of bodily harm to get the job done
Willingness to risk harm to yourself for someone else's sake
The ability to stand by the core values when it may not be the popular thing to do

questions.question: According to AFH 1, the intent of the Airman's Creed was to enhance the building of what among Airmen?
options: A sense of professionalism and discipline
A coherent bond and warrior ethos
An understanding of Air Force heritage
